title: pilocereus keyensis
intro: Pilocereus keyensis, also known as the "Key West Prickly Pear" or "Key Prickly Pear Cactus," is a species of cactus native to the Keys in Florida. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial compounds, which are believed to have various health benefits. The health benefits of Pilocereus keyensis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels and cholesterol. In herbal preparations, the plant's stems are typically harvested, dried, and made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
